item.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: OpenAI is getting serious about courting enterprise users. On Tuesday, the AI lab released a new set of capabilities for Codex, meant to expand the agentic tool’s uses in the workplace. Together with the new tools, the company released an internal report on how Codex is being used for knowledge work, finding its uses go […]

item.title: Board, the new game startup from Mirror founder Brynn Putnam, raises $20M, has already sold thousands
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Board, the startup building what it calls "together tech" designed to bring people into the same room, has closed a Series A led by Union Square Ventures.

item.title: Password manager Dashlane says hackers stole some customers’ password vaults
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The password manager giant said hackers were able to 'brute-force' its two-factor system, allowing them to access customer accounts and download their password vaults.

item.title: Turning your purse into a cyberdeck is the most fun way to resist big tech
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The women building these over-the-top, bedazzled cyberdecks aren’t in it for the glitter alone. This trend is reaching its peak at a time when people feel powerless against big tech.

item.title: How Europe’s AI strategy diverges from Silicon Valley’s
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: The global AI race is often framed as a battle between the United States and China. But at VivaTech, Europe is expected to make the case for an entirely different model.

item.title: Meta tests ‘Series’ for episodic Reels on Instagram and Facebook
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Meta told TechCrunch that it's considering ways to monetize the new feature, but didn't share specifics on what that could look like.

item.title: X caters to creators with new ‘React with Video’ feature
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X will now let you 'react with video' to posts.

item.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

item.title: Apple’s MacBook Neo is winning over a new generation of buyers
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The MacBook Neo shipped 1.1 million units in its first weeks on sale, IDC estimates, as Apple pushes deeper into the mainstream laptop market.

item.title: Amazon Prime Day 2026 takes place June 23-26
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Groceries and household essentials will be a main focus of this year's deals event.

item.title: Blue Origin plans to launch New Glenn again this year after explosion
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: CEO Dave Limp said damage to the company's launchpad in Florida was not as bad as expected. But Blue Origin still hasn't shared the cause of last week's explosion.

item.title: ZeroDrift raises $10M to protect AI models from themselves
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.

item.title: Rocket engine startup Impulse raises $500 million to hire people, not AI
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Engineering physical systems still depends on human talent, according to Impulse Space president Eric Romo.

item.title: Pacific Fusion’s latest prototype packs 440 gigawatts into an 80-nanosecond burst
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Pacific Fusion's sub-scale prototype delivered enormous amounts of power in a flash, setting the company up for its demonstration power plant.

item.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

item.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.

item.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: In a wild ride for 22-year-old founder and CEO Ethan Thornton, Mach Industries has raised another $300 million. It already has five autonomous vehicles in development and completed a major acquisition.

item.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

item.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

item.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.
